claims.claim: Hyperrectangular grid abstraction is a conservative overapproximation of the concrete MDP; safety in the abstract system implies safety in the concrete system, with monotone refinement as grid resolution increases

evidence.reference: Theorem 6 (State Abstraction Soundness), Section 4, tool_paper.tex lines 432-443; Theorem 7 (Monotone Refinement), lines 453-459
evidence.details: Proof by induction on time horizon H; full proof in Appendix B.1 (lines 1063-1090)

claims.claim: End-to-end safety probability decomposes as P(safe) >= 1 - eps_regime - eps_DAG - eps_invariance - eps_shield, replacing the monolithic two-term bound with per-stage error tracking

evidence.reference: Theorem 8 (Decomposed Composition), Section 5, tool_paper.tex lines 500-520; proof in Appendix lines 959-978

claims.claim: Four bounded liveness specifications (DrawdownRecovery, LossRecovery, PositionReduction, RegimeTransition) with G(trigger -> F[0,H] recovery) patterns, achieving zero false negatives across 100 trajectories

evidence.reference: Section 3.2 (Bounded Liveness Specifications), tool_paper.tex lines 339-368; Appendix E (lines 1286-1336)

claims.claim: Student-t emission model with Normal-Inverse-chi-squared prior substantially outperforms Gaussian emissions on heavy-tailed data (BIC 5552.26 vs 6131.21)
Safety tolerance delta is the most sensitive hyperparameter (PAC-Bayes bound 0.0397 at delta=0.01 to 0.0248 at delta=0.2); kappa, emission prior scale, prior count, and CI alpha are insensitive

evidence.reference: Section 6.4 (Prior Sensitivity Analysis), tool_paper.tex lines 698-719; Appendix D (lines 1125-1282)

claims.claim: Independent verifier re-derives PAC-Bayes bounds using only NumPy/SciPy (no causal_trading imports); mean discrepancy 0.113 between main and independent bounds across 10 trials

evidence.reference: Section 6.5 (Independent Verification), tool_paper.tex lines 752-776; Appendix G (lines 1402-1453)

claims.claim: Sound interval arithmetic with IEEE-754 machine-epsilon inflation bridges real-number proofs and floating-point computation

evidence.details: Interval arithmetic correctness validated via unit tests

claims.claim: PAC-Bayes Catoni bound is non-vacuous at all tested sample sizes: 0.00753 at n=100, monotonically decreasing to 0.000166 at n=10000
HSIC-based kernel independence tests detect nonlinear causal relationships missed by linear methods

evidence.details: Gaussian kernel, permutation/gamma tests, conditional HSIC via residualisation

claims.claim: HSIC-Lasso recovers all 3 nonlinear causal features (sin, squared, abs) vs 1/3 for standard LASSO
Sequential Causal Invariance Test (SCIT) with anytime-valid e-values controls type-I error at any stopping time
Posterior-predictive shield restricts actions to those satisfying bounded LTL safety specs with posterior probability >= 1 - delta

evidence.reference: Theorem 3 (Posterior Shield Soundness), Section 3, tool_paper.tex lines 327-337; proof in Appendix lines 899-914

claims.claim: Satisfaction probability is convex in transition probabilities for L_convex fragment; verification reduces to O(K^2) vertex checks

evidence.reference: Theorem 4 (Exact Vertex Verification), Section 3.3, tool_paper.tex lines 385-390; proof lines 917-945

claims.claim: P(safe) >= 1 - eps_1 - eps_2 when shield uses only invariant features

evidence.reference: Theorem 5 (Composition), Section 3.4, tool_paper.tex lines 394-401; proof lines 947-957

claims.claim: Invariant edge set E_inv is identifiable from observational data under faithfulness, minimum-regime-duration, and ANM independence conditions

evidence.reference: Theorem 1 (Identifiability), Section 2.1, tool_paper.tex lines 210-224; proof in Appendix lines 870-895

claims.claim: Sticky HDP-HMM with beam sampling infers number of regimes from data (K_max=5 practical cap)

Coupled EM loop has non-decreasing log-likelihood under exact E-step and PC-stable M-step, with contraction rate monitoring and multi-restart cross-comparison to detect and escape spurious fixed points
claims.caveat: Convergence is to a LOCAL optimum; global optimality not guaranteed. Multi-restart with contraction rate monitoring and cross-comparison mitigates local optima risk.

claims.claim: MCMC convergence diagnostics (R-hat, ESS, Geweke) verify posterior quality before trusting PAC-Bayes bounds

evidence.reference: Appendix H (MCMC Convergence Diagnostics), tool_paper.tex lines 1484-1504

claims.claim: Complete pipeline runs on single CPU; daily recalibration ~15 min, per-action shield check <1 ms
Framework generalizes across asset classes: equity (ARI=0.093), FX (ARI=0.861), crypto (ARI=0.483) with non-vacuous PAC-Bayes bounds and full shield permissivity in all cases
Multi-restart EM with contraction rate monitoring and spurious fixed-point detection; contraction rates converge below 1.0 across all asset classes (FX: 0.56, equity: 0.91, crypto: 0.94)

evidence.details: Contraction rate monitoring and multi-restart cross-comparison for spurious fixed-point detection

claims.claim: Adaptive delta adjustment based on posterior concentration improves shield permissivity from 0.1 to 0.333+ while maintaining safety guarantees

evidence.details: Adaptive delta parameter and graceful degradation method for improved shield permissivity

not_claimed: Real-money trading results — all experiments use synthetic data from known RI-SCMs
Human evaluation studies — no user studies conducted
PyPI package availability — not published to any package registry
GitHub repository availability — not hosted on any public repository
Support for LLMs or other AI models — this is a classical statistical/causal tool
Lean 4 formal proofs — theorems are proved on paper with Python implementations, not mechanised
Real market data results — synthetic data only with known ground truth
Performance guarantees on live markets — all guarantees are conditional on model-class assumptions
limitations: PAC-Bayes bounds assume exact Bayesian posterior; MCMC approximation introduces additional error not captured in the bound
EM convergence is to local optima only; global optimality not guaranteed despite multiple restarts
Convex LTL fragment L_convex is restrictive; specs with F (eventually) or U (until) outside the bounded pattern require conservative verification
K_max <= 5 regime cap is a practical constraint, not a theoretical necessity
Floating-point arithmetic may violate exact convexity assumed in proofs; interval arithmetic mitigates but does not eliminate this

Total decomposed error bound (1.602 in experiments) exceeds 1.0, meaning the union bound is vacuous in this regime — longer sequences or better regime detection needed for informative end-to-end bounds. However, adaptive delta adjustment improves shield permissivity from 0.1 to 0.333+, significantly reducing the shield error contribution
Independent verifier uses simpler bound computation, so discrepancy is expected; 'verified=false' reflects this methodological gap, not a soundness failure
All evaluation is on synthetic data; transferability to real financial data is unvalidated
